_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
min_doctor_qualification: MCh/ DNB/ Equivalent (Pediatric Surgery, Surgical Gastroenterology)
clinical_indications: Oesophageal stricture requiring mechanical dilation and threading for a period of six months or more, facilitated by gastrostomy.

preauth.label: Barium contrast swallow / Upper Gastrointestinal Endoscopy

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes - detailed history, signs & symptoms, planned line of treatment, indication for procedure?
Barium contrast swallow/Upper GI endoscopy report submitted?
Did the barium swallow test/upper GI endoscopy show constant narrowing on swallow with diagnosis of stricture?
Did the patient present with dysphagia for food or liquids?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and treatment details?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes available?
Is the discharge summary with follow-up advise provided?
Was the clinical evaluation and imaging indicative of surgery?

common_queries: Barium swallow test or upper GI endoscopy report did not show constant narrowing consistent with oesophageal stricture.
Patient's clinical presentation did not include dysphagia for food or liquids.
Missing or incomplete clinical notes detailing history, symptoms, planned treatment, or procedure indication.
Missing or incomplete detailed indoor case papers (ICPs) or procedure/operative notes at claim submission.

clinical_key_pointers: An oesophageal stricture refers to the abnormal narrowing of the oesophageal lumen, which requires treatment. It is a serious sequela to many different disease processes and underlying etiologies, particularly in children.
In neonatal period or early infancy, oesophageal strictures may manifest as slow feeding and excessive regurgitation with or without cyanotic episodes.
Older children with oesophageal strictures often present with foreign body impaction of food in the esophagus or complain of dysphagia with solids, requiring oral drinks to push food bolus.
surgical_options: Operations for Replacement of Oesophagus by Colon (Corrosive strictures)
